message:
fix(core): reject CREATE TABLE ... AS SELECT ... STRICT with NotImplemented

Guards the materialized CTAS path so a caller who passes
`CREATE TABLE copy AS SELECT ... STRICT` gets a structured
`FrankenError::NotImplemented("CREATE TABLE ... AS SELECT ... STRICT is not yet supported")`
instead of falling through and quietly building a non-STRICT table
under a STRICT-looking declaration. STRICT tables require
type-affinity enforcement that the materialize-from-rows helper
does not currently implement; until we wire that in, rejecting at
the connection boundary is the safe behaviour.

The check lives at the top of
`execute_materialized_create_table_as_select_statement`, before
`create.name.name` is cloned and before the schema borrow, so the
rejection happens with zero side effects on the database state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
